prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='THETA', seasonality_champion='ONLY_CWT_rf', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.32) and high seasonality variance (0.70), indicating models capture patterns very differently. The true series shows consistent upward trend across all folds, with THETA best tracking trends and ONLY_CWT_rf best capturing seasonality. Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists, suggesting horizon-specific selection would help. Given both high disagreement and high seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage complementary strengths while mitigating individual model weaknesses.

  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",

  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.32) and high seasonality variance (0.70), indicating models capture patterns very differently. The true series shows consistent upward trend across all folds, with THETA best tracking trends and ONLY_CWT_rf best capturing seasonality. Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists, suggesting horizon-specific selection would help. Given both high disagreement and high seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage complementary strengths while mitigating individual model weaknesses."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25", "best_per_horizon_by_validation"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.25}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=8 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity, favoring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread ratio=1.32 shows high model disagreement (DWT_catboost=15040 vs NaiveMovingAverage=31527), justifying DBA combination for phase-shift robustness. With n_windows=3 (few windows), small top_k=3 and shrinkage=0.25 provide stability. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination explicitly for high seasonality variance (0.70). best_per_horizon_by_validation captures early/late horizon specialists separately."
}
